---
title: "AI (Artificial Intelligence)"
subtitle: "Concepts, Environments, and Collaboration"
author: "Brian Yandell ([_byandell.github.io_](https://byandell.github.io))"
date: last-modified
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    embed-resources: true
    footer: "AI (Artificial Intelligence) | Concepts & Environments"
    highlight-style: github
---

# AI (Artificial Intelligence) Contents

- [Perspectives & Environmental Impact](#perspectives-environmental-impact)
- [AI Environments](#ai-environments)
- [Prompt & Context Engineering](#prompt-context-engineering)

# Perspectives & Environmental Impact {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## How Might We Think About AI?

### Tools Reshaping Us
> “We shape our tools and thereafter they shape us.”  
> &mdash; *Marshall McLuhan (via John Culkin)*

- Deeply engaging with AI is a process, not just pushing a button.
- Treat it as a collaboration rather than just a passive tool.

::: {.callout-note}
### Social Collaboration
Jaron Lanier's [There is no AI](https://byandell.github.io/Jaron-Lanier-There-is-no-AI/) reframes AI as a medium for human cooperation, emphasizing data dignity and creator credit.
:::

## The Disrupted & Rethought Workforce

### Workforce Transformation
- Early success is seen in AI-agent-native startups.
- Legacy companies experience shifts in workforce dynamics.
- Focus is shifting from replacing people to rethinking how we collaborate.

::: {.callout-tip}
### Core References
- **NYT**: 
  - [*Coding After Coders*](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/12/magazine/ai-coding-programming-jobs-claude-chatgpt.html) (Thompson)
  - [*The Workers Letting AI Do Their Jobs*](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/14/podcasts/the-daily/ai-coders.html) (Daily)
  - [*The Small-Business Owners Managing Armies of A.I. Employees*](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/04/magazine/ai-agents-openclaw-small-business.html) (Thompson)
- **Atlantic**:
  - [*AI Panic Cycle*](https://www.theatlantic.com/podcasts/2026/02/the-ai-panic-cycle-and-whats-actually-different-now/686077/) (Warzel)
  - [*What Do the People Building AI Believe?*](https://www.theatlantic.com/podcasts/2026/02/what-do-the-people-building-ai-believe/686173/) (Warzel)
:::

## Ethics & Environmental Impact

### Environmental Footprint
- Rapid growth of data centers strains local **power** and **water** resources.
- Efficiency of algorithms is improving, but resource demand is rising.
- Increasing focus on protecting natural resources and supporting indigenous lands.

::: {.callout-important}
### Call for Guardrails
Scientists warn in
[*Nature* that uncritical adoption of AI in science requires urgent regulatory guardrails](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-01557-x).
:::

**Ethics Resources**:

- [Pope and AI](https://byandell.github.io/The-Pope-and_AI/)
- [Ethics of AI (Tyson Swetnam)](https://tysonswetnam.com/blog/posts/2025-01-14-gpt101/#ethics-of-ai)
- [No Data Centers on Native Land](https://Honorearth.org/stopdatacolonialism)

## Data Center Resources & Efficiency

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Energy & Water Reports
- **UN Report**: Details footprints of carbon, water, and land usage.
- **Texas HARC & UT News**: Reviews data center boom and water usage in Texas.
- **LBNL**: U.S. Data Center Energy Usage Report.
- **Data Center Map**: Tracker for infrastructure.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Best Practices & Forecasts
- **Microsoft**: Sustainable datacenter designs using zero water for cooling.
- **Google**: Climate-conscious cooling commitments.
- **Salish Tribal Alliance**: Zero Edge cloud data sovereignty.
- **Micro Data Centers**: Shift toward Modular Edge Computing.
:::
:::

## AI & the Scientific Community

Gleanings from the [ESIIL Network Slack Channel](https://esiil.slack.com/archives/C03KLSEEYF8):

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
- [ESIIL AI Summit 2026](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/juanpedromaestre_esiil-2026-share-7463707566229991425-mPDk)
- [Algorithmic Monocultures in Hiring](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27371)
- [The Machine Consumes Itself](https://doi.org/10.1007/s42438-026-00666-0)
- [Ecology is not yet ready for AI](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2533976123)
- [Legacies of foundation species](https://doi.org/10.1126/sciadv.aef9983)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- [Ecological archives governance](https://eos.org/opinions/the-governance-gap-threatening-long-term-ecological-archives/)
- [Hallucinated references arXiv ban](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-01595-5)
- [AI for GHG Monitoring Webinar](https://www.nationalacademies.org/projects/DEPS-BEES-25-P-692/events/archive)
- [Accelerating scientific discovery with Co-Scientist](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-026-10644-y)
:::
:::

# AI Environments {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## What is an AI Environment?

### Setting for AI Systems to Operate & Interact

- **Components**: Hardware, software, data, and algorithms.
- **Types**: Physical (robots) or virtual (simulations, cloud platforms).
- **Feedback**: Allows real-time interaction and feedback with surroundings.
- **Machine Learning**: Enables adaptation and improvement over time.

::: {.callout-note}
### Applications
Autonomous vehicles, smart homes, virtual assistants, and connected IDEs.
:::

## Connected Development Environments

### Model Context Protocol (MCP)
- Open protocol introduced by Anthropic.
- Allows LLMs to interact securely with local/cloud tools and codebase context.
- Enables AI agents to read files, run commands, and edit code under user guidance.

::: {.callout-note}
### Interactive Workflow
Rather than copying and pasting code, the AI agent directly reads and modifies local code blocks based on natural conversations.
:::

## Cloud Educational Environments

Enable students and teams to build and test code without complex local setup:

- **GitHub Codespaces**: Cloud-hosted container integrated with GitHub and Copilot.
- **Google Colab**: Interactive Python/R notebooks with Gemini integrations.
- **CyVerse Discovery Environment**: Advanced biology and geospatial platform.
  - Integrates AI assistants (Roo / Cline) into its VS Code IDE workspace.
  - **AI Verde**: API tunnel providing access to high-performance LLMs.
- **NSF ACCESS**: High-throughput computing via Jetstream2, HTCondor, and PATh.

## Google Gemini & Antigravity

### Antigravity 2.0 Suite
Launched May 2026 with four primary tools:
- **Antigravity 2.0**: Multi-agentic code platform.
- **Antigravity CLI**: Command line terminal tool.
- **Antigravity SDK**: Building custom agent apps.
- **Antigravity IDE**: Workspace-integrated assistant.

::: {.callout-warning}
### Transition Note
IDE version 2.0 does not carry over history automatically. See [Recovering Antigravity Conversations](../AI/recover_conversations.html) for step-by-step help.
:::

**Gemini Resources**:

- [Prompt Design Strategies](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/prompt-design)
- [GitHub Repo Imports](https://kb.wisc.edu/ai/135575)

## Standalone AI IDEs & Tools

AI-native environments have quickly expanded beyond standard editor integrations:

- **[Claude Code](https://www.anthropic.com/product/code)**: Anthropic's terminal-based coding agent.
- **[OpenAI Codex](https://openai.com/index/openai-codex/)**: Deeply integrated code generation models.
- **[Positron](https://posit.co/products/open-source/positron/)**: Posit's next-generation IDE built for R and Python data science.
- **[Windsurf](https://secondtalent.com/insights/windsurf-review-2026-the-ai-ide-redefining-coding-workflows/)**: Standalone AI editor optimizing code editing workflows.
- **[Cursor](https://docs.cursor.sh/mcp)**: Advanced fork of VS Code featuring native MCP tool support.

::: {.callout-caution}
Be careful when using terminal-based interactive interpreters (like
[radian](../radian.html))
alongside background AI terminal execution.
:::

## Agentic AI & Multi-Agent Orchestration

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Orchestrating Agent Teams
- Modern developers orchestrate multiple agents to tackle complex tasks.
- Each agent holds its own role, persona, and technical skills.
- Commercial frameworks exist in Claude, ChatGPT, and Antigravity.
- **[SOUL.md](https://soul.md/)**: Markdown-based configuration files defining agent personalities.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Team Science Platforms
- **[OpenClaw](https://www.openclaw.ai/) &
[Hermes](https://hermes-agent.org/)**: Open orchestration engines.
- **[OASIS ScienceClaw](https://github.com/CU-ESIIL/openclaw_container) &
[ScienceClaw](https://github.com/beita6969/ScienceClaw)**: Forks of OpenClaw acting as digital twins for scientific working groups.
- **[Co-Scientist](https://deepmind.google/blog/co-scientist-a-multi-agent-ai-partner-to-accelerate-research/)**: AI agents working in teams (DeepMind) ([YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aSY_vFFmkW0))
- **[Claude Science](https://claude.com/product/claude-science)**: AI agents working in teams (Anthropic)
:::
:::

## Agentic AI References

- [Agentic AI](https://tyson-swetnam.github.io/intro-gpt/agentic/) (Swetnam)
- [Agent-Environment Interface in AI](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/artificial-intelligence/agent-environment-interface-in-ai/) (GeeksforGeeks)
- [Multi-Agent Orchestration Guide](https://www.agensi.io/learn/multi-agent-orchestration-guide) (Agensi)
- [Agentic Engineering](https://www.jayminwest.com/agentic-engineering-book/) (Jaymin West)
- [A multi-agent system for automating scientific discovery](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-026-10652-y) (Nature)
- [Digital twins as a tool for ecosystem research](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tree.2026.04.016) (Trends in Ecol & Evol)

## Large Language Models (LLMs)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Transformer Networks
- **Structure**: Massive transformer networks with nodes joined by weighted edges.
- **Weights**: Nodes/edges are fixed; weights are adjusted based on training data.
- **Sequence Prediction**: Learning patterns to predict the next token in a sequence.
- **Scale**: Billions or trillions of nodes to generate rich responses to prompts.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### LLM Explanations & Guides
- **[IBM: What are LLMs?](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/large-language-models)**
- **[GeeksforGeeks LLM Guide](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/artificial-intelligence/large-language-model-llm/)**
- **[AWS: What is an LLM?](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/large-language-model/)**
- **[Geospatial Harmonization with LLMs](https://github.com/CU-ESIIL/LLM_lesson_exemplar)** (CU-ESIIL)
:::
:::

## What are AI Tokens?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### How Models Process Language
- **Tokens**: Units of data representing words, subwords, images, or audio snippets.
- **Encoding**: Converting text to numerical embeddings that capture contextual meaning.
- **Contextualization**: Processing embeddings through layers to understand relationships.
- **Decoding**: Predicting and generating output tokens, then transforming them back to human text.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Tokenization Resources
- **[Encoder vs. Decoder Models](https://magazine.sebastianraschka.com/p/understanding-encoder-and-decoder)** (Sebastian Raschka)
- **[What Are AI Tokens?](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/ai-tokens-explained/)** (NVIDIA)
- **[How Models Read Text](https://aiinsightsnews.net/what-are-tokens-in-ai/)** (AI Insights)
- **[Tokens: GenAI Currency](https://www.sentisight.ai/tokens-explained-new-currency-of-generative-ai/)** (Sentisight)
- **[Tokenization Building Blocks](https://medium.com/data-science-collective/the-invisible-building-blocks-of-ai-what-you-need-to-know-about-tokenization-acadd86a63ba)** (Medium)
- **[gpt-tokenizer playground](https://gpt-tokenizer.dev/)**
:::
:::

## Mixture of Experts (MoE)

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Computational Efficiency
- **Conditional Routing**: Activates only a subset of network layers for each query rather than the whole model.
- **Expert Sub-networks**: Inputs are routed to specialized expert layers via a gating network.
- **Parameter Scaling**: Allows massive scaling of parameters without a proportional increase in compute cost.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### MoE Resources
- **[A Visual Guide to MoE](https://newsletter.maartengrootendorst.com/p/a-visual-guide-to-mixture-of-experts)** (Maarten Grootendorst)
- **[What is MoE?](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/nlp/what-is-mixture-of-experts-moe/)** (GeeksforGeeks)
- **[Mixture of Experts Blog](https://huggingface.co/blog/moe)** (Hugging Face)
- **[Mu & Lin (2026) paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.07137)** (arXiv)
:::
:::

## Open Source LLM Specifications

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Key Characteristics
- **Technical Specs**: Developer lineage (Google Gemma, Meta Llama, Qwen, GLM), context windows (32k to 1M+), training tokens.
- **Operational & Licensing**: Release dates, open vs. proprietary license types, quantization levels (FP16, INT8, 4-bit) defining memory/RAM needs.
- **Capabilities**: Benchmark scores (MMLU, GSM8K), text-only vs. multimodal (images, audio, video), language support.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Model Sources (AI Verde)
- **[glm-4.7-flash](https://ollama.com/library/glm-4.7-flash)** (Ollama)
- **[Gemma 3 12B](https://openrouter.ai/models/google/gemma-3-12b)** (OpenRouter)
- **[Phi-4](https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/models/nvidia/phi-4/overview)** (NVIDIA)
- **[Qwen3 Tech Report](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.09388)** (arXiv)
- **[MiniMax M2](https://openrouter.ai/models/minimax/minimax-m2)** (OpenRouter)
- **[gpt-oss-120b](https://openrouter.ai/models/gpt-oss-120b)** (OpenRouter)
- **[Verde Model Spec Catalog](https://github.com/byandell/Documentation/blob/main/verde_models.csv)**
:::
:::

## What is an AI Harness?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### The Harness Metaphor
- **Horse Metaphor**: Ethan Mollick compares an LLM to a powerful horse. Without a harness (bridle, reins, saddle), it has no direction or boundaries.
- **Harness Engineering**: Mitchell Hashimoto defines it as engineering solutions so an agent never repeats a mistake.
- **Normalizing Term**: Mollick organizes AI into *Models, Apps, and Harnesses*.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Core Origins & Analogies
- **[My AI Adoption Journey](https://mitchellh.com/writing/my-ai-adoption-journey)** (Mitchell Hashimoto)
- **[A Guide to Which AI to Use in the Agentic Era](https://www.oneusefulthing.org/p/a-guide-to-which-ai-to-use-in-the)** (Ethan Mollick)
- **Analogies**:
  - *"The model is commodity. The harness is the moat."* (Sarah Chen)
  - *"The model is the engine. The harness is the car."* (Grant Harvey)
:::
:::

## Harness Engineering References

- **[Complete Guide to Agent Harness](https://harness-engineering.ai/blog/agent-harness-complete-guide/)** (Sarah Chen, Harness Engineering)
- **[What is Harness Engineering?](https://atlan.com/know/what-is-harness-engineering/)** (Emily Winks, atlan)
- **[AI Harnesses and CLIs, Explained](https://www.theneuron.ai/explainer-articles/ai-harnesses-and-clis-explained-the-real-reason-everyones-talking-about-infrastructure/)** (Grant Harvey, eero)
- **[What is a Harness?](https://www.jayminwest.com/agentic-engineering-book/6-harnesses/1-what-is-a-harness)** (Jaymin West, Agentic Engineering)
- **[Harness Metaphor & Human-AI Relations](https://andrewmaynard.net/what-the-rapid-adoption-of-the-harness-metaphor-in-artificial-intelligence-reveals-about-how-we-conceptualize-human-ai-relations/)** (Andrew Maynard)

# Prompt & Context Engineering {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Prompt Engineering Fundamentals

### Documenting the Trail
- Prompt engineering designs inputs to guide an AI's output.
- Always save your prompts to a file (e.g., `prompts.md`) to retain your workflow.
- Ask the AI agent to update `prompts.md` and `walkthrough.md` as you code.

::: {.callout-tip}
### Core References
- **[AI Prompt Examples](../prompts/)**
- **[Prompting Guide](https://www.promptingguide.ai/)**
- **Tyson Swetnam's [Getting Started with Prompts](https://tyson-swetnam.github.io/intro-gpt/prompts/#getting-started-basic-prompt-structure)**
:::

## Prompts to Organize Workflows

Split complex workflows into human-sized, manageable chunks:

1. **Extract Functions**: Move heavy operations to a helper R file (`functions.R`).
2. **Setup Notebook**: Create a clean Quarto document (`workflow.qmd`) that sources `functions.R`.
3. **Handle Deliverables**: Configure the workflow to output figures and data tables (PDF/CSV).
4. **Build Documentation**: Present the workflow in clear source and rendered forms.

::: {.callout-note}
Check out ESIIL's Three Rivers working group [AI Prompt Engineering docs](https://github.com/CU-ESIIL/resilience-rare-hydrologic-events-management-innovation-summit-2025__15/blob/main/docs/index.md#ai-prompt-engineering) for examples.
:::

## Context Engineering

### Managing Attention Budgets
- Every turn fills the agent's **context window**.
- Excessive background context or giant documents cause hallucination or crash.
- **Best Practice**: Request the agent to summarize files, then save and pass the summary to subsequent chats.

::: {.callout-important}
### Context Inflation
Avoid oversized onboarding files. Keep repository rules focused to preserve conversation token budget.
:::

## Context: `AGENTS.md` vs `Agent Skills`

AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Antigravity, Cursor) leverage two core standards to organize context:

| Feature | [AGENTS.md](https://AGENTS.md) | Agent Skills ([SKILL.md](https://agentskills.io)) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Purpose** | **Static repository context** (coding standards, project layout). | **Procedural knowledge** and automation recipes (deployment, tests). |
| **Scope** | Bound to a single repository. | Reusable/portable across projects. |
| **Structure** | Single Markdown file in root. | Folder with `SKILL.md` + scripts. |
| **Trigger** | Loaded automatically. | Activated dynamically on keywords. |

## `AGENTS.md` (Repository Context)

Acts as the onboarding handbook for an AI agent entering a repository:

- **Directory Layout**: Highlights where components, hooks, or styles live.
- **Design & Coding Standards**: Strict rules (e.g., *"Prefer existing `Button` components"*).
- **Layered Rules**: Nested `AGENTS.md` can layer constraints on specific sub-folders.

### Real-world Examples:
- [OASIS Group AGENTS.md](https://github.com/CU-ESIIL/Working_group_OASIS/blob/main/AGENTS.md)
- [LLM Lesson Exemplar AGENTS.md](https://github.com/CU-ESIIL/LLM_lesson_exemplar/blob/main/AGENTS.md)
- [OASIS ScienceClaw AGENTS.md](https://github.com/CU-ESIIL/openclaw_container/blob/main/AGENTS.md)

## Agent Skills & `SKILL.md`

Agent Skills represent a portable toolbox extending what an agent can perform:

* **Trigger Actions**: YAML frontmatter defines triggers for specific command hooks.
* **TDD Workflows**: Anti-rationalization rules prevent agents from skipping steps (e.g. bypassing test suites).
* **Automation Scripts**: Scripts (Node, Python, Bash) execute complex deployment routines.

```markdown
---
name: vercel-deploy
description: Deploy my web app to Vercel production.
---
# Deploy Skill
...
```
